methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $5.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.8B - $312.5B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Palantir
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $312.5B

private.name: Crusoe Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $10B+

private.name: Ayar Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $3.75B

data.narrative:
SandboxAQ is a Series E AI & Software company founded by Jack Hidary, having raised $950M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 comparables—1 public (Palantir) and 8 private firms spanning the frontier AI sector, including Crusoe Energy, Ayar Labs, Waymo, and Figure AI.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression anchors to the company's last post-money valuation of $5.8B and applies a typical 1.4x step-up multiplier for Series E progression, implying $8.0B. Peer-median analysis references three same-sector, same-stage comparables: Palantir at $312.5B, Crusoe Energy at $10.0B, and Ayar Labs at $3.8B. The median of this set is $10.0B, though the range ($3.8B–$312.5B) reflects significant dispersion driven by Palantir's scale outlier position. Both methods weight equally in the triangulation.

Synthesizing these approaches yields a valuation range of $6.8B (low) to $11.5B (high), with a median implied value of $9.0B. This range sits modestly above the current $5.75B post-money and reflects stage-typical expansion. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the Palantir comp—whose $312.5B valuation reflects public-market premium and established revenue scale—and the homogeneity of the broader peer set, which spans heterogeneous business models within frontier AI.
